title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Command Retrieval
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
Calculate read, write, and execute effects for owner, group, and other on regular files and directories.
Translate symbolic and octal chmod operations while preserving unrelated bits and verifying the result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: User Group Identity · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

Which action demonstrates User Group Identity without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.

content.text: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which User Group Identity condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

hints: Name the User Group Identity precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A username alone determines every permission decision.

options.text: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.
A username alone determines every permission decision.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

stimulus.title: Special Bits Acls case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: An ACL entry always grants its listed permissions regardless of the ACL mask.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient. Artifact under review: a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Special Bits Acls, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Special Bits Acls: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
